content.problemStatement:
Given two undirected binary trees T1 and T2, check if they are the identical or not.

Two binary trees are considered identical if they are structurally same, and the nodes have the same value.

For example, the following two binary trees are identical:




Input Format

The first line of the input contains two space separated integers NNN and MMM — the number of nodes in the two binary trees.
Next N−1N - 1N−1 lines contain three space separated characters p1i,c1i,Rip1_i, c1_i, R_ip1i​,c1i​,Ri​, describing the first tree with edge informations - p1ip1_ip1i​ is an integer denoting the parent node of the iiith edge, c1ic1_ic1i​ is child node, and the letter RiR_iRi​ denotes whether the child is left child or right child, it's value is `L` if c1ic1_ic1i​ is left child of p1ip1_ip1i​, else `R`.
Similarly, next M−1M - 1M−1 lines contain three space separated characters p2i,c2i,Rip2_i, c2_i, R_ip2i​,c2i​,Ri​, describing the second tree with edge informations - p2ip2_ip2i​ is an integer denoting the parent node of the iiith edge, c2ic2_ic2i​ is child node, and the letter RiR_iRi​ denotes whether the child is left child or right child, it's value is `L` if c2ic2_ic2i​ is left child of p2ip2_ip2i​, else `R`.


Output Format
Output on the single line, `YES` if the two trees are identical else `NO` otherwise.
constraints: All p1i's and c1i's are distinct
content.inputFormat: The first line contains two space-separated integers N and M, the number of nodes in the two binary trees. Next N-1 lines contain three space-separated integers p1i, c1i, and Ri, describing the first tree with edge information - p1i is an integer denoting the parent node of the i-th edge, c1i is the child node, and the letter Ri denotes whether the child is the left child or right child, it's value is 'L' if c1i is the left child of p1i, else 'R'. Similarly, next M-1 lines contain three space-separated integers p2i, c2i, and Ri, describing the second tree with edge information - p2i is an integer denoting the parent node of the i-th edge, c2i is the child node, and the letter Ri denotes whether the child is the left child or right child, it's value is 'L' if c2i is the left child of p2i, else 'R'.
content.outputFormat: Output on a single line, 'YES' if the two trees are identical, else 'NO' otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The two trees are identical because they have the same structure and node values.
The two trees are identical because they have the same structure and node values.
The two trees are not identical because they have different structures.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can create two binary trees based on the given edge information and then check if the two trees are identical by comparing their structures and node values. We can use a recursive approach to traverse the trees and compare their nodes.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N + M) per test case, where N and M are the number of nodes in the two binary trees.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N + M) for storing the two binary trees.
